phenotypes.label: Metabolic syndrome and related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights metabolic syndrome as a central theme along with related conditions such as obesity, hypertension, and fatigue. Patients represented may show a clustering of metabolic disorders and common symptoms like fatigue and musculoskeletal pain, suggesting a complex interplay of chronic conditions.

phenotypes.label: Aortic and thoracic conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses conditions primarily related to aortic abnormalities, such as aneurysms and ectasia of the thoracic aorta. It also includes gastrointestinal and systemic conditions that may present with related symptoms, suggesting a patient population with complex comorbidities around vascular and gastrointestinal health.

phenotypes.label: Benign prostatic hyperplasia disorders
phenotypes.description: This cluster includes conditions primarily associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), such as outflow obstruction, urinary tract obstruction, and erectile dysfunction. Patients often present with various urinary symptoms related to BPH, which can significantly affect their quality of life.

phenotypes.label: Gastro-esophageal reflux-related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights conditions related to gastro-esophageal reflux disease, particularly its complications like esophagitis. It also includes other diverse conditions such as liver fat accumulation and lung abnormalities, suggesting a heterogeneous patient population with multi-system involvement.

phenotypes.label: Lung and skin malignancies
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses conditions primarily focused on malignant melanoma and various abnormal lung findings, suggesting a cohort of patients with significant respiratory and dermatological concerns. The presence of lung nodules and diagnostic imaging abnormalities indicates potential malignancy, while the inclusion of disorders related to the pancreas and kidney hints at a more complex clinical picture that may affect treatment considerations.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid and metabolic conditions
phenotypes.description: This cluster includes conditions related to thyroid dysfunction such as acquired hypothyroidism and autoimmune thyroiditis, paired with metabolic issues like megaloblastic anemia and vitamin deficiencies. Patients may present with interrelated endocrine and metabolic challenges, emphasizing the importance of monitoring thyroid function alongside metabolic health.

phenotypes.label: Posttraumatic stress and dependence
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses a cluster of conditions predominantly associated with posttraumatic stress disorder and various forms of substance dependence, including alcohol and nicotine. The co-occurrence of severe and recurrent major depression further highlights the significant psychological distress experienced by these patients, suggesting a need for comprehensive mental health support.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The conditions represented in this topic span a wide range of clinical themes, including malignancies, hypertension, and various systemic and skin conditions. There is no cohesive clinical narrative connecting these conditions, making it difficult to derive specific patient patterns or cohort insights.

phenotypes.label: Attention and mood disorders
phenotypes.description: This topic clusters around attention and mood disorders including ADHD, childhood emotional disorders, dysthymia, and depressive disorders. Patients represented by this topic are likely dealing with significant emotional and behavioral challenges, often requiring coordinated management strategies.

phenotypes.label: Chronic musculoskeletal pain
phenotypes.description: This topic includes conditions related to chronic musculoskeletal pain such as low back pain, neck pain, and various forms of lumbar and hip pain. The presence of multiple related pain conditions suggests a patient population that may be experiencing significant and overlapping discomfort across different body regions.

phenotypes.label: Gastrointestinal symptoms and conditions
phenotypes.description: This cluster primarily includes gastrointestinal symptoms such as abdominal distension, flatulence, and constipation, indicating patients may be dealing with various digestive issues. It also captures related conditions like disorders of the artery and psychotic disorders, reflecting a broader impact on health that may involve abdominal pain and psychosomatic symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Mitral and aortic valve disorders
phenotypes.description: This topic centers around cardiovascular conditions, particularly disorders involving the mitral and aortic valves, alongside associated symptoms like palpitations and dyspnea. Patients in this group often present with heart murmurs and may have related conditions such as pulmonary hypertension or cardiomegaly, indicating a significant impact on heart function.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This topic includes a range of conditions that do not share a common clinical theme. It features topics like kidney stones and various eye disorders, which suggests a lack of cohesive clinical relevance in terms of patient presentation or management.

phenotypes.label: Cerebrovascular disease cluster
phenotypes.description: This cluster primarily focuses on conditions related to cerebrovascular events, such as cerebral infarctions due to thrombosis and transient ischemia. Patients within this group may present with neurological deficits like slurred speech or ataxia, suggesting a significant impact on their functional abilities and indicating potential vulnerabilities in vascular health.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions include entheseopathy, neurological disorders, and respiratory diseases, suggesting no coherent clinical theme. This varied array lacks a shared story across the conditions, making it difficult to derive any meaningful insights for cohort selection or exclusion.

phenotypes.label: Breast cancer and related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses conditions primarily related to breast cancer, including malignant neoplasm of the female breast and associated diagnostic findings. It also includes genetic predispositions and various disorders of the breast, highlighting the complexity of managing breast health in affected patients.

phenotypes.label: Venous disorders and complications
phenotypes.description: This topic centers around a cluster of venous conditions primarily affecting the lower extremities, such as peripheral venous insufficiency and varicose veins. Patients may present with symptoms including localized edema and pain, indicating potential complications like deep vein thrombosis and lymphedema.

phenotypes.label: Ocular conditions and glaucoma
phenotypes.description: This cluster encompasses various ocular conditions, primarily focusing on glaucoma and cataracts. The presence of multiple forms of glaucoma and associated conditions indicates a patient population at risk for progressive vision impairment and possibly blindness if not managed appropriately.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy-related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encapsulates various aspects and complications of pregnancy. Conditions identified include complications during different trimesters, high-risk situations, and other pregnancy disorders, reflecting a focus on maternal health during gestation.

phenotypes.label: Neurological movement disorders
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a cluster of neurological conditions characterized by abnormal motor function and cognitive impairment. Key conditions such as epilepsy, seizures, and Parkinson's disease suggest that patients may experience both involuntary movements and cognitive challenges, reflecting a complex neurological profile.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: This topic comprises a disparate collection of conditions, including endometriosis, liver transplants, and epilepsy, without a cohesive clinical theme. The lack of meaningful patient clusters suggests that it may not be clinically useful for identifying patient populations or guiding care decisions.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions show no shared clinical theme, representing disparate diagnoses ranging from hormonal disorders to mental health issues. This lack of cohesion makes it difficult to derive a meaningful clinical interpretation.

phenotypes.label: Substance use and respiratory issues
phenotypes.description: This topic represents a cluster of conditions primarily related to respiratory issues like pneumothorax and pleural effusion, intertwined with serious mental health challenges including suicidal ideation and substance abuse. Patients bearing these conditions may exhibit complex clinical presentations requiring careful evaluation and management.

phenotypes.label: Neuromuscular and endocrine disorders
phenotypes.description: This group highlights various conditions affecting the nervous system and endocrine function, including arthropathy, mononeuritis, and thyroid disorders. The presence of these conditions suggests a focus on patients with multifaceted health issues related to both the nervous and hormonal systems.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The topic encompasses disparate conditions such as contact dermatitis, acquired hypothyroidism, and essential hypertension, along with various symptoms like nausea and chest pain. There is no clear shared clinical theme among these conditions, making it difficult to identify a specific patient group or clinical scenario. Therefore, this topic lacks clinical utility and remains mixed in nature.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions represent a mix of gastrointestinal issues, eye disorders, mental health conditions, and others. There is no coherent clinical theme or unifying patient profile, making it difficult to derive meaningful insights from this group.

phenotypes.label: Vision and sexual health issues
phenotypes.description: This topic clusters conditions related to sexual health, specifically erectile dysfunction, alongside various eye-related conditions like hypermetropia, presbyopia, and retinopathy associated with diabetes. Such a combination suggests a focus on the interplay between sexual health and eye diseases, potentially in a population with multiple comorbidities or complications for shared cardiovascular risks.

phenotypes.label: Ocular and cardiovascular complications
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses various conditions related to significant eye health issues, particularly retinal detachment and diabetes-related retinopathy, alongside associated systemic conditions like heart disease. The clustering suggests a patient demographic with complex interactions between ocular health and systemic diseases, particularly among those with chronic conditions such as diabetes and cardiovascular issues.

phenotypes.label: Urinary tract conditions cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic centers around various urinary tract conditions, including urinary tract infections and neurogenic bladder dysfunction. The presence of these conditions suggests a population affected by a range of urinary issues, often linked by underlying bladder dysfunction or infection-related complications.

phenotypes.label: Complex cancer and effusion conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic reflects a cluster of serious medical conditions, primarily focusing on acquired organ absence, malignant neoplasms, and various types of effusions. It suggests a patient population with advanced disease presentations and significant comorbidity between cancer-related conditions and associated complications.

phenotypes.label: Lipid and blood disorder cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic primarily focuses on conditions related to lipid metabolism disorders and various blood coagulation disorders. The presence of hernias and other gastrointestinal issues suggests a diverse patient population experiencing complications related to these primary themes.

phenotypes.label: Metabolic and cardiovascular conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a cluster of interrelated conditions primarily associated with metabolic syndrome and cardiovascular disease. The presence of hypertension, atherosclerosis, and type 2 diabetes suggests patients may be at increased risk for significant complications related to heart health and glucose metabolism.

phenotypes.label: Liver disease spectrum
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a range of liver conditions, prominently featuring cirrhosis and hepatitis C. Patients associated with these conditions may experience significant complications, including thrombocytopenia and ascites, indicative of advanced liver disease and portal hypertension.

phenotypes.label: Complications of medical interventions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses conditions related to complications arising from medical and surgical interventions, including dysphonia, dysphagia, and various late effects. It reflects the clinical challenges faced by patients experiencing complications after treatments, highlighting the need for careful monitoring and management in post-procedural care.

phenotypes.label: Rheumatoid arthritis and complications
phenotypes.description: This cluster revolves around rheumatoid arthritis and its associated conditions, including seropositive variants and other complications like pulmonary embolism and osteoporosis. The presence of both autoimmune and vascular issues suggests these patients may have systemic involvement, highlighting an important clinical relationship between joint and vascular health.

phenotypes.label: Neurological and behavioral conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic represents a cluster of neurological and behavioral conditions, with Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder and Multiple Sclerosis as prominent conditions. The presence of mood disorders and anxiety indicates a potential overlap in the associated mental health challenges these patients may experience.

phenotypes.label: Foot and ankle pain cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a variety of conditions related to foot and ankle discomfort, including arthralgia, plantar fascial issues, and osteoarthritis. Patients in this cluster often present with localized pain in the foot or ankle, suggesting potential musculoskeletal concerns that may require further evaluation.

phenotypes.label: HIV and associated conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses conditions related to human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection, including herpesvirus infections and other dermatological issues. It highlights the clinical complexities faced by patients with HIV and potential co-existing health issues.

phenotypes.label: Pancytopenia and associated symptoms
phenotypes.description: This topic features pancytopenia as a central condition, with a notable presence of symptoms such as nosebleeds and thrombocytopenic disorders. This cluster suggests patients may have underlying hematological issues, leading to a range of clinical findings including bleeding tendencies and potential immune dysfunction.

phenotypes.label: Kidney transplant-related complications
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights conditions frequently associated with patients who have undergone kidney transplantation, such as end-stage renal disease and chronic kidney disease. The presence of gastrointestinal symptoms like diarrhea, constipation, and nausea suggests these patients may experience significant digestive challenges, possibly related to their renal status or treatments.

phenotypes.label: Neurological disorders and symptoms
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses a range of neurological conditions and associated symptoms, highlighting issues such as disorders of the brain, swelling, and hydrocephalus. The presence of migraines and depressive episodes suggests a population dealing with significant neurological and psychological challenges.

phenotypes.label: Respiratory and pancreatic conditions
phenotypes.description: This cluster encompasses various conditions affecting both the respiratory and pancreatic systems, including bronchiectasis, pneumonia, and disorders of the pancreas. Patients represented may have overlapping respiratory symptoms such as dyspnea and cough, which are critical for assessing their overall health status.

phenotypes.label: Chronic gastrointestinal and mental health
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a cluster of conditions associated with gastrointestinal issues, such as gastroesophageal reflux disease and epigastric pain, along with mental health disorders like anxiety and depression. The presence of obesity-related conditions further emphasizes the interconnected nature of physical and psychological health in affected patients.

phenotypes.label: Chronic lung disease and comorbidities
phenotypes.description: This topic centers around chronic obstructive lung disease and its significant association with other conditions such as HIV, immunodeficiency disorders, and mental health issues like bipolar disorder and schizophrenia. Patients represented in this cluster often have complex health needs, particularly involving respiratory issues and potential substance abuse, highlighting the importance of comprehensive management strategies.

phenotypes.label: Recurrent depression and anxiety
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a pattern of recurrent major depressive episodes alongside generalized anxiety disorder, emphasizing the struggles patients face with both conditions. The prominence of remission states indicates a focus on fluctuating mental health challenges, suggesting ongoing management needs for these patients.

phenotypes.label: Inflammatory bowel disorders
phenotypes.description: This topic primarily covers inflammatory bowel diseases, especially ulcerative colitis and related intestinal disorders. Patients in this cluster often experience gastrointestinal issues, such as hemorrhage, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, indicating the complexity of their conditions and potential complications.

phenotypes.label: Postprocedural complications and findings
phenotypes.description: This cluster highlights various complications and abnormal findings following surgical or procedural interventions. The presence of conditions such as postprocedural states, complications of procedures, and radiology results suggests a focus on patient outcomes and recovery issues related to recent medical interventions.

phenotypes.label: Knee osteoarthritis and related pain
phenotypes.description: This topic revolves around knee osteoarthritis and its associated conditions, including joint pain and injuries. Patients likely experience significant discomfort stemming from degenerative joint disease, with conditions such as meniscus tears contributing to their overall clinical picture.

phenotypes.label: Gastrointestinal inflammatory conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses a range of gastrointestinal inflammatory disorders, including Crohn's disease and abscesses, highlighting the complex relationship between these conditions. It also features related issues such as bowel obstruction and incontinence, suggesting a cohort of patients with significant digestive and systemic challenges.

phenotypes.label: Neurological and muscular conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic represents a cluster of neurological and muscular conditions including dysarthria, dysphagia, and nystagmus, suggesting patients may present with various speech and swallowing difficulties alongside muscular weakness and coordination issues. The presence of conditions like asthenia and obstructive sleep apnea also indicates potential fatigue and respiratory problems in this patient group.

phenotypes.label: Skin lesions and changes
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses various skin conditions such as actinic keratosis, senile hyperkeratosis, and melanocytic nevi, suggesting a focus on lesions and neoplasms of the skin often associated with chronic sun exposure and aging. The presence of multiple benign skin conditions indicates a clinical concern for dermatological assessment and management in older populations or those with significant sun exposure histories.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid and related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic reflects a focus on thyroid disorders, particularly thyrotoxicosis and hypothyroidism, indicating a clinical concern for metabolic regulation and its systemic effects. Additionally, it encompasses various conditions that may be secondary or related to thyroid dysfunction, such as cardiovascular issues and mental health disorders.

phenotypes.label: Shoulder pain and related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses various shoulder-related conditions including shoulder joint pain, rotator cuff tears, and osteoarthritis. The presence of Crohn's disease suggests possible gastrointestinal implications in some patients, while the inclusion of primary malignant neoplasm of the respiratory tract indicates a diverse patient population with multiple comorbidities. This reflects a clinical pattern focused on shoulder disorders and chronic pain experiences.

phenotypes.label: Psychotic disorders and comorbidities
phenotypes.description: This topic clusters around psychotic disorders such as schizoaffective disorder and schizophrenia, indicating patients with serious mental health conditions. Additionally, there are several comorbidities, including hyperlipidemia and hypertension, which suggest a complex clinical profile often seen in these patients.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This topic includes a range of conditions without a clear, shared clinical theme. The leading conditions span sexual activity status, eye disorders, and metabolic concerns such as prediabetes, suggesting a lack of cohesive clinical interpretation.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: This topic includes a mix of conditions related to different organ systems such as breast, liver, and renal issues, but lacks a coherent clinical theme. The presence of disparate conditions like fibrocystic disease and liver disease suggests varied patient presentations without a unifying clinical story.

phenotypes.label: Cognitive and mood disorders cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses various cognitive and mood disorders, prominently featuring dementia and bipolar disorder. The presence of anxiety and organic mental disorders suggests a significant overlap in patients experiencing mental health challenges alongside cognitive impairments.

phenotypes.label: Sleep disorders and pain issues
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses conditions related to sleep problems such as sleep apnea and obstructive sleep apnea syndrome. It also includes various pain-related conditions like backache, migraine, and other types of headaches, indicating a potential cluster of patients experiencing both sleep disturbances and pain issues.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid conditions cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic revolves around various thyroid conditions, particularly postoperative hypothyroidism and malignant neoplasms of the thyroid gland. It indicates that patients are likely experiencing complications related to thyroid dysfunction as well as associated conditions like hypocalcemia and vitamin D deficiency.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The conditions in this topic span various themes including mental health (anxiety and depression), gynecological issues (uterine leiomyoma and menstrual disorders), and respiratory conditions (asthma and upper respiratory infections). There is no coherent clinical narrative connecting these diverse conditions, making this topic difficult to interpret meaningfully for patient cohorts.

phenotypes.label: Menopause-related health issues
phenotypes.description: This cluster highlights health conditions associated with menopause, including menopausal syndrome and postmenopausal bleeding. They suggest a focus on women's health issues during and after the transition to menopause, along with prevalent concerns regarding hearing loss and related symptoms in this demographic.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The conditions in this topic range from dermatological issues like acne and its variants to mental health disorders, including bipolar disorder and anxiety. There is no clear overarching clinical theme unifying these varied conditions, making it challenging to derive specific clinical insights.

phenotypes.label: Allergic rhinitis and asthma cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a group of related respiratory conditions, primarily allergic rhinitis, chronic sinusitis, and various forms of asthma. The presence of these conditions suggests that patients may experience overlapping symptoms, especially related to allergy and respiratory health, indicating a significant impact on their quality of life.

phenotypes.label: Urinary incontinence disorders
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses various forms of urinary incontinence, including female stress incontinence, urge incontinence, and mixed types. It suggests a cohort of patients experiencing significant distress related to bladder control issues, often linked to pelvic floor disorders and associated urogenital symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Kidney transplant and complications
phenotypes.description: This topic primarily focuses on conditions associated with kidney transplants and their complications. Key issues include end-stage renal disease, chronic kidney disease, and associated conditions like anemia and hyperparathyroidism, which highlight the complex management needs of these patients.

phenotypes.label: Sleep disorders and related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses various sleep disorders, primarily focusing on hypersomnia and obstructive sleep apnea syndrome, along with related conditions like restless legs and migraines. Patients represented by this pattern may experience significant challenges related to their sleep quality and attendant symptoms, impacting their daily functioning and overall health.

phenotypes.label: Chronic metabolic syndrome cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic reflects a cluster of conditions often associated with metabolic syndrome, including type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. Patients with these conditions typically share risk factors and experiences, suggesting a common underlying clinical theme related to metabolic dysregulation and cardiovascular risk.

phenotypes.label: Neurological and dermatological conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses a range of neurological and dermatological conditions, prominently featuring essential tremor and benign neoplasms of the colon. The presence of disparate conditions like residual hemorrhoidal skin tags suggests varied patient presentations, but overall, there's no clear single clinical narrative among the leading conditions.

phenotypes.label: Diabetes and prostate cancer cluster
phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a notable association between prostate cancer and Type 1 diabetes, suggesting a unique patient population that may experience both conditions. The presence of related complications such as hyperglycemia and gastroparesis further indicates complex management considerations for these patients.

phenotypes.label: Vascular and infection-related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic clusters conditions related to vascular issues, such as embolism, alongside various infections, including acute vaginitis and urinary tract infections. Patients represented by this theme may display a range of acute presentations linked to thrombotic events and infectious processes.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis and related conditions
phenotypes.description: This topic reflects a cluster of skin and joint conditions, primarily centered around psoriasis and its variations, including psoriasis with arthropathy. It also includes chronic fatigue syndrome and other inflammatory conditions, suggesting a connection between dermatological and systemic inflammatory disorders in this patient population.

phenotypes.label: Nail and mobility disorders
phenotypes.description: This topic covers conditions related to nail and walking difficulties, including dystrophia unguium and ingrowing nails, alongside mobility issues like difficulty walking. Additionally, it includes mood disorders and hypertension, suggesting that patients may present with a complex interplay of physical and mental health challenges.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The topic contains a variety of conditions, including herpes zoster, dysphagia, and diaphragmatic hernia. There is no coherent clinical theme among the leading conditions, making it difficult to derive meaningful insights about patient populations or presentations.

phenotypes.description: This topic encompasses a range of acute symptoms including pain, nausea, and shortness of breath. The presence of multiple gastrointestinal and respiratory symptoms suggests patients may be experiencing significant distress, potentially indicative of a variety of underlying acute conditions.

phenotypes.label: Sleep disorders with hearing issues
phenotypes.description: This topic represents a cluster of conditions primarily involving sleep disorders, particularly insomnia and its variants, alongside various types of hearing loss. The co-occurrence of these conditions suggests a population of patients who may have overlapping challenges related to sleep quality and auditory function.

phenotypes.label: Autoimmune condition cluster
phenotypes.description: This cluster represents a group of autoimmune conditions, prominently featuring systemic lupus erythematosus and Sjögren's syndrome. Patients may present with overlapping symptoms affecting multiple systems, including joint pain, lung complications, and skin manifestations.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions in this topic span a variety of unrelated clinical themes, including liver disease, vitamin deficiencies, skin disorders, and joint issues. phenotypes.description: This topic highlights a clinical pattern involving severe obesity, schizoaffective disorder, and various forms of tobacco dependence. Patients exhibiting these conditions may face complex interactions between psychological health and obesity, necessitating careful management of both physical and mental health.

phenotypes.description: The leading conditions in this topic cover a wide range of clinical manifestations including varicella, various neurological disorders, and endocrine issues among others.
